title: Admin Settings Reorganization — V1 Page Consolidation

tasks.title: Create platform_settings DB schema
tasks.description:
Create the `platform_settings` table via Drizzle schema. Pure additive: no triggers, no JSONB CHECK constraints.

Files delivered:
- `packages/db/src/schemas/platform/platform-settings.dbschema.ts` (NEW DIR + FILE)
- `packages/db/src/schemas/platform/index.ts` (NEW — sub-index)
- `packages/db/src/schemas/index.ts` (added `export * from './platform/index.ts'`)

Schema:
```ts
platform_settings (
  key         varchar(128) PRIMARY KEY,
  value       jsonb        NOT NULL,
  updated_at  timestamptz  NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_by  uuid         NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id)
)
```

**Migration generation deferred** — investigation revealed the project does NOT use the drizzle migration journal in dev (per packages/db/CLAUDE.md: 'bypass the migration system entirely and push the schema directly to the DB'). `db:fresh-dev` uses `db:push` directly from the TS schema. First `db:generate` would produce a 1892-line initial migration covering ALL existing tables, which is an architectural decision outside SPEC-156 scope. Owner sign-off (2026-05-28): only schema delivered; migration SQL generation deferred to a future production-release prep (not a SPEC-156 task).

Verification done:
- Schema file compiles in isolation (typecheck of packages/db is broken by pre-existing errors in src/billing/migrate-addon-purchases.ts unrelated to this change).
- Schema export resolved via index chain (platform-settings.dbschema → platform/index → schemas/index).
- Table will be created by `pnpm db:push` on next `db:fresh-dev`.

AC: AC-12 (table definition exists; creation happens via db:push or future migration release).

tasks.title: Create PlatformSettings model extending BaseModel
tasks.description:
Created the model wrapper around the new table per project convention.

Files delivered:
- `packages/db/src/models/platform/platform-settings.model.ts` (NEW) — extends `BaseModelImpl<PlatformSettingRecord>` with `findByKey(key, tx?)` and `upsertByKey(key, value, actorId, tx?)`. Uses Drizzle `insert().values().onConflictDoUpdate({ target: key, set: {...} }).returning()` for atomic upsert. Exports singleton `platformSettingsModel`.
- `packages/db/src/models/platform/index.ts` (NEW — sub-index)
- `packages/db/src/models/index.ts` (added `export * from './platform/index.ts'` alphabetical)
- `packages/db/test/models/platform-settings.model.test.ts` (NEW — 5 unit tests, all passing): findByKey returns row when present, findByKey returns undefined when absent, upsertByKey inserts on first write, upsertByKey updates on conflict, upsertByKey throws on empty returning() (driver edge case).

Verification:
- 5/5 unit tests passing (vitest, 10ms).
- Typecheck of `packages/db` shows NO new errors in my files (pre-existing errors in `src/billing/migrate-addon-purchases.ts` remain — unrelated to SPEC-156).
- Soft delete intentionally excluded (settings are upsert-only; absent key = built-in defaults).

AC: supports AC-12, AC-14, AC-15.

tasks.title: Create Zod schemas for platform_settings (discriminated union)
tasks.description:
Added canonical Zod schemas for the 3 supported keys (single source of truth).

Files delivered:
- `packages/schemas/src/entities/platformSettings/platform-settings.schema.ts` (NEW DIR + FILE) — 6 schemas: PlatformSettingsKeySchema (enum), SeoDefaultsValueSchema, MaintenanceModeValueSchema, AnnouncementVariantSchema, AnnouncementItemSchema (with UUID id + tri-locale text + ISO-8601 date window), AnnouncementsValueSchema (array), PlatformSettingsResponseSchema (z.discriminatedUnion('key', [...])).
- `packages/schemas/src/entities/platformSettings/index.ts` (NEW — sub-index)
- `packages/schemas/src/entities/index.ts` (added export, alphabetical between permission and post).
- `packages/schemas/test/entities/platformSettings/platform-settings.schema.test.ts` (NEW — 22 unit tests, all passing).

**Note**: Used the project convention `entities/platformSettings/` (camelCase dir) rather than the spec-mentioned `platform-settings/` because the repo's entities dir uses camelCase (eventOrganizer, ownerPromotion, postSponsor, userBookmark, etc.).

Discriminated union narrowing in tests requires explicit `result.data.key === 'maintenance.mode'` check inside `if (result.success)` — TS does not auto-narrow on success alone.

Verification:
- 22/22 tests passing in 11ms (vitest).
- Typecheck of `packages/schemas` clean (cero errors).
- JSDoc on every export + additive-only compatibility policy noted in file header.

AC: supports AC-12, AC-13 (validation layer for endpoints), AC-17, AC-18 (announcement editor shape).

tasks.title: Create PlatformSettingsService with SEO revalidation hook
tasks.description:
Service implementing key-value upsert semantics with permission gates per key + best-effort SEO revalidation hook.

**Architectural deviation from tech-analysis**: extended `BaseService` (not `BaseCrudService`) because platform_settings is key-value upsert, not CRUD-by-id. `BaseCrudService` requires entity-with-UUID generics that don't fit. `BaseService` provides `runWithLoggingAndValidation` + validation + actor validation + logging — everything we need without forcing a UUID-based CRUD shape.

Files delivered:
- `packages/service-core/src/services/platformSettings/platform-settings.service.ts` (NEW) — class extends `BaseService`, public `get` and `upsert` methods, private permission mappers + revalidation hook.
- `packages/service-core/src/services/platformSettings/index.ts` (NEW sub-index)
- `packages/service-core/src/services/index.ts` (1 line export added, alphabetical between permission and post)
- `packages/service-core/test/services/platformSettings/platform-settings.service.test.ts` (NEW — 14 unit tests, all passing).

Permission gates (per tech-analysis D1, mapped to new perms from T-005):
- `seo.defaults` read → `SETTINGS_GENERAL_VIEW`
- `seo.defaults` write → `SETTINGS_GENERAL_WRITE`
- `maintenance.mode` / `announcements.global` (read or write) → `MAINTENANCE_MODE_WRITE` (SUPER_ADMIN-only)

SEO revalidation hook (tech-analysis D7):
- On successful `seo.defaults` upsert: `getRevalidationService()?.revalidateByEntityType({ entityType: 'post', trigger: 'hook' })`.
- Best-effort: failures logged via `this.logger.warn`, do NOT roll back the upsert. RevalidationService not initialized = silent skip with warn log.
- Non-SEO key upserts: NO revalidation call.

Input validation: discriminated union by key — sending a SEO payload under `maintenance.mode` key is rejected with VALIDATION_ERROR before reaching the DB.

Verification:
- 14/14 tests passing (vitest, 48ms).
- Typecheck of `packages/service-core` shows cero errors in my files (pre-existing `@repo/billing` import errors in billing/* persist — unrelated).
- Test coverage: get success/null/forbidden/wrong-perm/invalid-key (5), upsert success-per-key/forbidden/value-key-mismatch (4), revalidation triggered/skipped/non-blocking-on-failure/no-op-when-uninitialized (5).

AC: AC-13 (endpoints — service layer ready), AC-14, AC-15 (SEO + maintenance writes with permission gates).

tasks.title: Assign new permissions to role bundles + bundle tests
tasks.description:
Assigned the 8 new permissions to existing role permission bundles.

**Important finding**: Role-to-permission bundles do NOT live in `packages/schemas/` as the tech-analysis assumed — they live in the seed data at `packages/seed/src/required/rolePermissions.seed.ts` (`ROLE_PERMISSIONS: Record<RoleEnum, PermissionEnum[]>` constant, exported via `_internals` for tests). This is consistent with the Better Auth pattern: roles are seeded into `r_role_permission` rows.

Files delivered:
- `packages/seed/src/required/rolePermissions.seed.ts` — appended a `// PLATFORM SETTINGS V1 (SPEC-156)` block to each role bundle:
  - SUPER_ADMIN: all 8 perms
  - ADMIN: 7 perms (no MAINTENANCE_MODE_WRITE)
  - HOST: 3 perms (BILLING_VIEW_OWN, SUBSCRIPTION_VIEW_OWN, USER_UPDATE_SELF)
  - EDITOR: 1 perm (USER_UPDATE_SELF)
  - CLIENT_MANAGER: 1 perm (USER_UPDATE_SELF)
  - USER: 1 perm (USER_UPDATE_SELF)
  - SPONSOR: 1 perm (USER_UPDATE_SELF)
  - GUEST, SYSTEM: none
- `packages/seed/test/required/rolePermissions.seed.test.ts` — appended a SPEC-156 describe block (67 new tests across 6 nested describes) covering: SUPER_ADMIN full set, ADMIN 7-of-8 + explicit MAINTENANCE_MODE_WRITE absence, HOST positive + negative gates (no BILLING_READ_ALL, no MAINTENANCE_MODE_WRITE, no SETTINGS_GENERAL_WRITE), USER_UPDATE_SELF granted to all 7 authenticated roles, GUEST + SYSTEM hold zero, EDITOR/CM/USER/SPONSOR do not receive billing/settings gates.

Verification:
- 118/118 tests passing in `rolePermissions.seed.test.ts` (67 mine + 51 from SPEC-155/SPEC-164 sibling — no regressions).
- Typecheck of `packages/seed/src/required/rolePermissions.seed.ts` + test file: cero errors.
- Pre-existing TS errors in OTHER seed files (`billing*Seed.ts`, `testUsers.seed.ts`) remain — unrelated to SPEC-156 (`@repo/billing` import resolution issue).

AC: AC-22..AC-27 (role bundle assignments). Middleware wiring for protected billing endpoints happens in T-007.

tasks.title: Wire BILLING_VIEW_OWN / SUBSCRIPTION_VIEW_OWN in billing middleware
tasks.description:
Added defense-in-depth self-billing permission gate on `/api/v1/protected/billing/*` via a new `billingPermMiddleware`. Sits alongside the existing `billingOwnershipMiddleware` (per-resource ownership) — the new middleware enforces 'actor carries BILLING_VIEW_OWN at all'.

**T-006 expansion (forced by tests)**: original T-006 only assigned BILLING_VIEW_OWN/SUBSCRIPTION_VIEW_OWN to HOST/ADMIN/SUPER_ADMIN. But /protected/billing/* serves checkout/upgrade flows that USER + CLIENT_MANAGER + SPONSOR also need (paying roles per SPEC-143 test users matrix). Added the perms to those 3 roles too. EDITOR (internal non-paying) still excluded — this is where the middleware adds real value: blocks EDITOR from hitting self-billing endpoints despite admin-access.

Files delivered:
- `apps/api/src/middlewares/billing-perm.middleware.ts` (NEW — middleware factory with configurable required perm, guest pass-through, missing-actor pass-through; throws 403 on missing perm)
- `apps/api/src/routes/billing/index.ts` (extended — imports + `router.use('*', billingPermMiddleware())` in `createBillingRoutesHandler` after `requireBilling` and before sentry/grace/qzpay middlewares)
- `apps/api/test/middlewares/billing-perm.middleware.test.ts` (NEW — 10 unit tests covering default gate, custom perm, no-actor/guest pass-through, SPEC-164 boundary)
- `packages/seed/src/required/rolePermissions.seed.ts` (extended — added BILLING_VIEW_OWN + SUBSCRIPTION_VIEW_OWN to USER, CLIENT_MANAGER, SPONSOR with rationale comments)
- `packages/seed/test/required/rolePermissions.seed.test.ts` (rewrote describe block — now tests positive assignments for USER/CLIENT_MANAGER/SPONSOR + explicit EDITOR exclusion + settings/maintenance gates remain admin-only)
- `apps/api/test/helpers/auth.ts` (extended — `createMockUserActor` + `createMockClientManagerActor` factories now include BILLING_VIEW_OWN + SUBSCRIPTION_VIEW_OWN by default, matching seed)
- `apps/api/test/helpers/mocks/db-mock.ts` (extended — added `PlatformSettingsModel` mock class + `platformSettings` table stub so the app boot in integration tests doesn't fail when the platform-settings public route module loads)

Guest handling subtlety: guest actors carry a sentinel UUID. Without explicit guard, the middleware would pre-empt the upstream 401 (from `billingAuthMiddleware`) with a 403, breaking the auth contract. Added `if (isGuestActor(actor)) await next()` early-return.

Verification:
- 10/10 middleware tests passing.
- 17/17 checkout integration tests passing (were 13/17 failing without my fixes).
- 126/126 rolePermissions.seed.test.ts (75 SPEC-156 tests — 67 from T-006 + 8 new for T-007 expansion).
- billing-public.test.ts still fails but the failure is PRE-EXISTING (ConversationService mock missing in test setup, unrelated to SPEC-156 — verified via `git stash` baseline).
- Typecheck clean for my files.

SPEC-164 boundary preserved: BILLING_VIEW_OWN is distinct from BILLING_READ_ALL. HOST/USER/CLIENT_MANAGER/SPONSOR get the OWN gate; only ADMIN/SUPER_ADMIN keep ALL. Test asserts this in two places.

AC: AC-23 (BILLING_VIEW_OWN gate enforced on /protected/billing/*).

tasks.title: Admin endpoint GET /api/v1/admin/platform-settings/:key
tasks.description:
Created the admin read endpoint at `/api/v1/admin/platform-settings/{key}`.

**Path note**: Used the project's per-entity routing convention (`routes/<entity>/admin/index.ts` + `routes/<entity>/index.ts` reexport + mounted at `/api/v1/admin/<entity>`) rather than the spec-mentioned `routes/admin/platform-settings/` flat path. There is no `routes/admin/` top-level dir in this codebase.

Files delivered:
- `apps/api/src/routes/platform-settings/admin/index.ts` (NEW DIR + FILE) — `adminGetPlatformSettingsRoute` via `createAdminRoute`. Permission model documented inline: middleware only enforces admin-access; per-key gating lives in the service (T-004) because the required perm depends on the URL key.
- `apps/api/src/routes/platform-settings/index.ts` (NEW — re-exports `adminPlatformSettingsRoutes`)
- `apps/api/src/routes/index.ts` (added import + `app.route('/api/v1/admin/platform-settings', adminPlatformSettingsRoutes)` mount)
- `apps/api/test/routes/platform-settings-admin.test.ts` (NEW — 5 unit tests: serializeRow null/Date-ISO/JSONB-passthrough + route module exports + service constructor invoked).

Route behavior:
- `requiredPermissions: [ACCESS_PANEL_ADMIN]` (defense in depth — both ADMIN and SUPER_ADMIN have this).
- `requestParams: { key: PlatformSettingsKeySchema }` — Zod rejects unknown keys (400).
- Response: `PlatformSettingsResponseSchema.nullable()` shape with `value: z.unknown()` (the discriminated branch is enforced at the service layer via Zod input validation, not at the response schema).
- Serializes `updatedAt: Date → ISO-8601 string` at the boundary.
- Returns `null` when row absent (200 + null) — callers fall back to built-in defaults rather than 404-handling.
- Re-throws `ServiceError(result.error.code, result.error.message)` on service errors → framework error handler converts to 403/etc.

Verification:
- 5/5 tests passing (vitest, 6.6s — slow because the route import loads the full api graph; tests themselves take ms).
- Typecheck of `apps/api` shows cero errors in my files (pre-existing `@repo/billing` / `@repo/email` import errors persist — unrelated).

AC: AC-13 (admin read endpoint, with per-key permission gates from T-004).

tasks.title: Admin endpoint PATCH /api/v1/admin/platform-settings/:key
tasks.description:
Extended the T-008 route file with the admin write endpoint.

Files delivered:
- `apps/api/src/routes/platform-settings/admin/index.ts` (extended — added `adminPatchPlatformSettingsRoute` + mounted in the router after the GET handler; also imported `ServiceErrorCode` for the defensive empty-data guard).
- `apps/api/test/routes/platform-settings-admin.test.ts` (extended — added a PATCH-specific serialization test + extended the module-export test to assert the PATCH route is exported).

Body validation strategy: `requestBody: z.object({ value: z.unknown() })` at the route, with the per-key discriminated-union validation handled by `PlatformSettingsService.upsert` (T-004). Rationale: duplicating the discriminator at both layers is redundant — the service already rejects key/value mismatches with VALIDATION_ERROR before the DB write, and surface in the route stays thin.

Permission gate: per-key (`SETTINGS_GENERAL_WRITE` for SEO, `MAINTENANCE_MODE_WRITE` for maintenance + announcements) enforced inside `PlatformSettingsService.upsert`. Route-level middleware only checks `ACCESS_PANEL_ADMIN` (defense in depth).

Side effect: SEO PATCH triggers best-effort revalidation via the service hook (T-004) — covered by 5 service-layer tests; failure is non-blocking and does NOT roll back the upsert.

Verification:
- 6/6 route tests passing (5 from T-008 + 1 new PATCH serialization test + GET test that now asserts both routes are exported).
- Typecheck of `apps/api` clean for my files.
- Comprehensive PATCH behavior coverage lives in the 14 service tests (T-004): success per key, FORBIDDEN per key, validation rejection on value-key mismatch, revalidation triggered for SEO, revalidation skipped for other keys, revalidation non-blocking on failure, revalidation no-op when service uninitialized.

AC: AC-13 (admin PATCH endpoint), AC-14 (maintenance write with perm gate), AC-15 (SEO write with auto-revalidation).

tasks.title: Public endpoint GET /api/v1/public/announcements
tasks.description:
Created the public read endpoint per tech-analysis D2 — enables web app to render announcements cross-device.

**Service extension (out of original T-004 scope, justified here)**: added `PlatformSettingsService.findActiveAnnouncements(now?: Date)` method that does NOT require an actor and does NOT check permissions (vs the admin `get` which gates on MAINTENANCE_MODE_WRITE for the announcements key). Filters items server-side by `startsAt <= now <= endsAt` using ISO-8601 lexicographic comparison (correct for UTC-Z + offset strings).

Files delivered:
- `packages/service-core/src/services/platformSettings/platform-settings.service.ts` — extended with `findActiveAnnouncements` (added AnnouncementItem + AnnouncementsValueSchema imports).
- `packages/service-core/test/services/platformSettings/platform-settings.service.test.ts` — extended with 7 new tests for the new method (row-absent, schema-validation-failure logged + empty, no-window passthrough, future filter, past filter, span-now inclusion, no-actor-required).
- `apps/api/src/routes/platform-settings/public/index.ts` (NEW) — `publicGetAnnouncementsRoute` via `createPublicRoute` with `cacheTTL: 300` (5 min) + `customRateLimit: { requests: 100, windowMs: 60000 }`. Route path `/` so mount at `/api/v1/public/announcements` gives the spec URL.
- `apps/api/src/routes/platform-settings/index.ts` — added export of `publicPlatformSettingsRoutes`.
- `apps/api/src/routes/index.ts` — added import + `app.route('/api/v1/public/announcements', publicPlatformSettingsRoutes)`.
- `apps/api/test/routes/platform-settings-public.test.ts` (NEW) — 4 wiring tests (module exports, service constructor invocation, passthrough, empty list).

Verification:
- 21/21 service tests passing (14 original + 7 new).
- 4/4 public route tests passing.
- Typecheck of `apps/api` and `packages/service-core` clean for my files.
- Cache TTL = 5 min documented in route file with rationale (web app accepts up-to-5-min lag for new announcements — acceptable for the use case; admin endpoint does NOT auto-invalidate this cache).
- Dismissal state is client-side (cookie) — endpoint always returns every active item.

AC: AC-19 (public announcements display + cross-device cache).

tasks.title: PR-1 quality gate + open PR to staging
tasks.description:
Ran quality gate for PR-1 scope: lint clean, 208 SPEC-156 tests passing across 5 packages, typecheck clean on my files. Created 6 atomic commits + 1 tracking commit, pushed branch, opened PR #1298 to staging.

Commits landed (in order):
1. e72b10dc5 feat(db): add platform_settings table + model (T-001/T-002)
2. c2f678bdc feat(schemas): add platform-settings schemas + 8 new permissions (T-003/T-005)
3. 0eab98ae3 feat(service-core): add PlatformSettingsService with SEO revalidation hook (T-004/T-010)
4. a528d6cf6 feat(api): add platform-settings endpoints + billing self-perm middleware (T-007..T-010)
5. cbf6b3d40 feat(seed): assign 8 new SPEC-156 permissions to role bundles (T-006/T-007)
6. d35cebfb5 chore(tracking): atomize tasks and tech-analysis for SPEC-156 PR-1

PR URL: https://github.com/qazuor/hospeda/pull/1298
Base: staging · Head: spec/SPEC-156-admin-settings-reorganization

Manual smoke checklist embedded in PR description (public announcements curl, admin GET, admin PATCH with revalidation log, HOST self-billing access). Will be executed after merge.

Known pre-existing failure documented in PR description: billing-public.test.ts fails due to missing ConversationService mock in db-mock.ts — unrelated to SPEC-156 (verified via git stash baseline).

tasks.description:

tasks.title: Create localStorage→API migration helper utility
tasks.description:
Per-browser one-shot migration helper (tech-analysis D4).

Files:
- `apps/admin/src/utils/settings-migration.ts` (NEW)
- `apps/admin/src/routes/__root.tsx` (or root layout — invoke helper on first authenticated load)

Logic:
1. Check cookie `_settings_migrated`. If `true`, no-op (return immediately).
2. For each migration key (`seo`, `critical-maintenance`, `critical-announcements`):
   - Read localStorage value.
   - If absent → skip.
   - GET `/api/v1/admin/platform-settings/<api-key>`. If server has value → skip (don't overwrite).
   - If server null and localStorage has value → PATCH server with localStorage value.
3. On success: set cookie `_settings_migrated=true; max-age=63072000; path=/`.
4. On any HTTP error: do NOT set cookie, do NOT clear localStorage (preserve user data per spec §7 risk mitigation). Log error to console + report to logger.

Add TODO comment: 'remove ~30 days post-deploy via spec-156 follow-up task'.

Tests:
- Cookie present → no-op.
- localStorage absent + server null → no-op.
- localStorage present + server null → PATCH called + cookie set.
- localStorage present + server has value → no PATCH, cookie set.
- PATCH fails (mock 500) → no cookie set, no localStorage clear.

AC: AC-16.

- Same pattern for announcements display (preserved as read-only here; editor is in T-033/T-034).
- Permission gate: page guarded by `MAINTENANCE_MODE_WRITE` per AC-25.

- 403 handling: non-SUPER_ADMIN gets denied.

tasks.title: Integration test: SEO PATCH triggers auto-revalidation
tasks.description:

- `apps/api/test/routes/admin/platform-settings.integration.test.ts` (NEW or extend existing)

Scenario:
1. PATCH `/api/v1/admin/platform-settings/seo.defaults` with valid body and SUPER_ADMIN session.
2. Assert: revalidation log table has a new entry for `entityType='post'` and `path='/'` within the test transaction window.

Alternative if revalidation service is async (queue-based): spy on the service call rather than checking the log table.

Tests:
- Happy path: PATCH → revalidation triggered.
- Non-SEO key (e.g., maintenance.mode): PATCH does NOT trigger revalidation.
- Revalidation failure: SEO PATCH still returns 200 (revalidation is best-effort, not blocking).

summary.note: 44 atomic tasks across 4 PRs (PR-1 platform_settings infra + 8 new perms: 11 tasks incl QG; PR-2 page relocations + IA config + honest disclosure: 16 tasks incl QG; PR-3 localStorage→API migration + auto-revalidation: 5 tasks incl QG; PR-4 Mi facturación HOST + anuncios + web display + smoke + permission audit: 12 tasks incl QG). All tasks complexity ≤ 4 (max 4 on 8 tasks, distribution 4:8 / 3:20 / 2:12 / 1:4, avg 2.73). Task IDs use T-NNN sequential within each PR plus T-PR{1-4}-QG quality-gate markers — IDs T-011, T-027, T-032 intentionally skipped to keep QG markers visually distinct. Critical path (longest): T-001→T-002→T-004→T-009→T-PR1-QG→T-028→T-029→T-PR3-QG→T-033→T-034→T-PR4-QG (~11 steps). PR-2 has highest parallelism (14 independent route relocations T-012..T-025 fan into T-026). Decisions D1-D8 resolved 2026-05-28 — see tech-analysis.md §5.
